articles.title: ED breaks locks to enter Abhishek Banerjee's residence during overnight raid
articles.description: A joint police team, including central forces, conducted an overnight search at Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee's Kolkata residence. The operation, linked to a Paschim Medinipur case, involved breaking locks after no response from occupants. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee rushed to the scene amidst the ensuing political controversy.

articles.title: IND vs AFG: Virat, Hardik injuries open doors for Ishan Kishan and Nitish Reddy
articles.description: Virat Kohli and Hardik Pandya's injuries have forced India into transition mode as they begin preparations for the 2027 ODI World Cup. Ishan Kishan appears set to get the No. 3 role, Nitish Kumar Reddy could replace Hardik, while young pacers Prince and Gurnoor await opportunities.
articles.full_text: Pratyush Raj is a cricket journalist with TOI Sports, specializing in all things cricket, both on and off the field. He began his career with the Business Standard before following his passion as a sports reporter for the Times of India in Chandigarh. Pratyush has also contributed to the sports coverage of India Today Group and his last stint was with The Indian Express. His love for sports was nurtured during his upbringing in flood-prone Saharsa, a district in North Bihar, where Cricket Samrat was his constant companion. Read More

articles.title: 1,569 days and counting: Russia-Ukraine war now longer than World War I
articles.description: The Russia-Ukraine war has surpassed World War I in duration, now exceeding 1,569 days. What began as a swift Russian operation has become Europe's longest and bloodiest conflict since WWII, defying early predictions. Despite diplomatic efforts and Western support for Ukraine, a durable peace remains elusive, with no clear end in sight.

articles.title: From Lebanon ceasefire to nuclear talks: What are the terms proposed by Iran in 'Islamabad Agreement'
articles.description: Iran has outlined conditions for a potential US agreement, prioritizing sanctions relief and maritime access over immediate nuclear program discussions. Tehran insists on lifting the US naval blockade and a new arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz, while also seeking to include Lebanon in the deal.

articles.title: 'Driven to London by masked men': Indian-origin man Gagandeep Singh sentenced to 34-years for kidnaping, raping woman
articles.description: An Indian-origin man, Gagandeep Singh, has been sentenced to 34 years in prison in the UK for kidnapping, torturing, and raping a woman. The 34-year-old was convicted of multiple serious offenses after a trial. He will serve 28 years in custody, followed by an extended license period, and will be deported after completing his sentence.

articles.title: Paraguay's president will be at USA's FIFA World Cup 2026 opener, but is Donald Trump attending?
articles.description: As the United States prepare to open their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign against Paraguay, attention has shifted off the pitch after Paraguayan president Santiago Peña confirmed he will attend the match in Los Angeles while US president Donald Trump will not. Trump instead offered support via a phone call to the squad, adding a political subplot to a high-profile opener between the hosts and returning South Americans.

articles.title: Tata Group tells Air India to focus on cutting losses
articles.description: The carrier is also reevaluating plans to fly to new domestic and international destinations, pruning some routes and postponing launches at some airports, such as the new Noida International Airport, the people familiar with the matter said. Air India’s cowed ambitions follow a series of challenges that have pushed it deep into the red.

articles.title: Despite rocky year, Tata Sons records high profit growth
articles.description: Tata Sons is understood to have recorded high-teens profit growth in FY26, a result that strengthens Chandrasekaran’s hand as he faces questions from Noel Tata, chairman of principal shareholder Tata Trusts, over losses at the company’s new businesses, including Tata Digital. Growth came despite lower dividend income from operating companies, including a reduced payout from TCS.

articles.title: Retail inflation inches up to 5-month high in May
articles.description: Despite the staggered increase in retail fuel prices during the past month in the backdrop of persisting conflict in West Asia, fuel inflation remained contained at 0.8% in May, though marginally higher than 0.7% in April. Economists expect the pass through to become visible in June. Dipti Deshpande, principal economist at Crisil said food inflation rose due to persistent high temperatures, which drove up prices of vegetables, dairy products and eggs.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: NTA issues clarification on CUET PG 2026 rescheduled exams, says no score normalisation applied
articles.description: The National Testing Agency has clarified CUET PG 2026 exam scheduling and score normalisation. A small number of candidates took a rescheduled exam due to unforeseen circumstances. NTA confirmed that all scores are absolute marks and no normalisation was applied. This decision ensures fairness for all candidates. The agency remains committed to transparent examinations.
articles.full_text: The National Testing Agency has clarified CUET PG 2026 exam scheduling and score normalisation. A small number of candidates took a rescheduled exam due to unforeseen circumstances. NTA confirmed that all scores are absolute marks and no normalisation was applied. This decision ensures fairness for all candidates. The agency remains committed to transparent examinations.

articles.title: Central Bank of India Apprentice recruitment 2026: Notification released for 4,500 posts; check eligibility, direct link
articles.description: The Central Bank of India has announced 4,500 apprentice vacancies for the 2026-27 financial year, with online applications open from June 12 to June 22, 2026. The selection process includes an online exam, local language test, document verification, and medical assessment, with a monthly stipend of Rs 15,000 for the 12-month training period.
articles.full_text: The Central Bank of India has announced 4,500 apprentice vacancies for the 2026-27 financial year, with online applications open from June 12 to June 22, 2026. The selection process includes an online exam, local language test, document verification, and medical assessment, with a monthly stipend of Rs 15,000 for the 12-month training period.

articles.title: RRB NTPC Graduate CBT 2 exam date 2026 announced: Check schedule, admit card details
articles.description: Railway Recruitment Boards have announced the tentative schedule for the RRB NTPC Graduate CBT 2 examination, set to be conducted on July 10, 2026. Candidates who cleared the first stage will be eligible. Exam city slips will be available 10 days prior, with admit cards released four days before the exam. Aadhaar verification is mandatory at the centre.

articles.title: IIT Kanpur opens walk-in admissions for PhD in Aerospace Engineering: Check how to apply
articles.description: IIT Kanpur's Aerospace Engineering department is inviting applications for its PhD Walk-in Admissions Programme 2026, with registration closing on June 16. Eligible candidates from CFTIs and top-ranked non-CFTI institutions can apply for research opportunities. Online interviews are scheduled for June 18, and selected scholars may receive a scholarship of up to Rs 42,000 per month.

articles.title: Angelina and Brad's son Knox drops 'Pitt' from surname
articles.description: Youngest son Knox Jolie has dropped 'Pitt' from his surname on his high school diploma, joining siblings Maddox, Pax, Shiloh, and Zahara in a similar move. This surname change trend among Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt's children follows their parents' ongoing divorce proceedings. Sources offer conflicting views on Jolie's alleged influence versus the children's autonomy.

articles.title: 'Mollywood Times' faces CBFC heat
articles.description: Mollywood Times, starring Naslen, faces a CBFC probe over alleged discrepancies between its certified and theatrical versions. Reports suggest muted expletives in the submitted copy were audible in cinemas, potentially violating certification rules. The board is investigating, with possible legal action and penalties for the producer if a breach is confirmed. Re-certification remains uncertain.

articles.title: Harry Styles pays tribute to David Hockney
articles.description: During his spectacular concert at Wembley Stadium, Harry Styles paid tribute to the legendary artist David Hockney. As the crowd roared, a touching quote from Hockney about the communal spirit of art flashed on the screen, setting the stage for a heartfelt performance. Earlier this year, Hockney captured Styles in a stunning portrait, emphasizing their artistic connection.

articles.title: 'Governor' BO Day 1: Manoj Bajpayee's film earns Rs 90 lakh
articles.description: June 12, 2026, saw the theatrical debut of Manoj Bajpayee's intriguing political drama 'Governor: The Silent Saviour.' Despite the odds, including fierce competition from various films, it earned Rs 0.9 crore on its premiere day. Directed by Chinmay Mandlekar, the film powerfully illustrates the struggles of an RBI governor navigating through the stormy seas of India's financial crisis. Read on to know more in detail.
